questions.question: Simplify: $\left(\frac{125}{512}\right)^{-\frac{2}{3}}$
questions.explanation: Flip the base: $\left(\frac{512}{125}\right)^{\frac{2}{3}}$. Cube root: $\frac{8}{5}$. Square: $\left(\frac{8}{5}\right)^2 = \frac{64}{25}$.

questions.question: Simplify: $\left(\frac{8}{27}\right)^{-\frac{2}{3}}$
questions.explanation: Flip the base: $\left(\frac{27}{8}\right)^{\frac{2}{3}}$. Cube root: $\frac{3}{2}$. Square: $\frac{9}{4}$.

questions.question: Simplify: $\left(\frac{16}{81}\right)^{-\frac{3}{4}}$
questions.explanation: Flip the base: $\left(\frac{81}{16}\right)^{\frac{3}{4}}$. Fourth root: $\frac{3}{2}$. Cube: $\frac{27}{8}$.

questions.question: Simplify: $\left(\frac{1}{8}\right)^{-\frac{4}{3}}$
questions.explanation: Flip the base: $8^{\frac{4}{3}}$. Cube root of 8 is 2. $2^4 = 16$.

questions.question: Simplify: $\left(\frac{27}{64}\right)^{-\frac{2}{3}}$
questions.explanation: Flip the base: $\left(\frac{64}{27}\right)^{\frac{2}{3}}$. Cube root: $\frac{4}{3}$. Square: $\frac{16}{9}$.

questions.question: Simplify: $\left(\frac{3}{5}\right)^{-4}$
questions.explanation: Flip the base and raise to the 4th: $\left(\frac{5}{3}\right)^4 = \frac{625}{81}$.

questions.question: Simplify: $\left(\frac{32}{243}\right)^{-\frac{4}{5}}$
questions.explanation: Flip the base: $\left(\frac{243}{32}\right)^{\frac{4}{5}}$. Fifth root: $\frac{3}{2}$. Raise to 4th: $\frac{81}{16}$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered simplifying expressions with negative rational exponents and fraction bases.
scoreMessages.70: Good work. Remember: a negative exponent means flip the base. The denominator of the exponent is the root, and the numerator is the power.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing. Focus on: 1) Flipping the fraction first when the exponent is negative, 2) Taking the root (denominator) before the power (numerator), 3) Simplifying completely.
scoreMessages.0: Start by: 1) Handling the negative sign by flipping the base, 2) Taking the root indicated by the denominator of the exponent, 3) Raising the result to the power indicated by the numerator.
